title: App Version Consistency Across SHC (operational inventory)
spl:
index=inventory sourcetype="splunk:shc_app_inventory"
| stats values(app_version) as ver by app_name, member
| eventstats dc(ver) as ver_count by app_name
| where ver_count > 1
| table app_name, member, ver
description: Mixed app versions on SHC members cause bundle skew and intermittent UI or search errors.
value: Mixed app versions on SHC members cause bundle skew and intermittent UI or search errors.
implementation: Push inventory script via `runshellscript` or external job. Alert on mismatch for production apps. Exclude dev-only apps via lookup.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SHC, REST.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `| rest /services/apps/local` per member, `sourcetype=splunk:shc_app_inventory`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Push inventory script via `runshellscript` or external job. Alert on mismatch for production apps. Exclude dev-only apps via lookup.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=inventory sourcetype="splunk:shc_app_inventory"
| stats values(app_version) as ver by app_name, member
| eventstats dc(ver) as ver_count by app_name
| where ver_count > 1
| table app_name, member, ver
```

Understanding this SPL

**App Version Consistency Across SHC (operational inventory)** — Mixed app versions on SHC members cause bundle skew and intermittent UI or search errors.

Documented **Data sources**: `| rest /services/apps/local` per member, `sourcetype=splunk:shc_app_inventory`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SHC, REST. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: inventory; **sourcetype**: splunk:shc_app_inventory. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=inventory, sourcetype="splunk:shc_app_inventory". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_name, member** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where ver_count > 1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **App Version Consistency Across SHC (operational inventory)**): table app_name, member, ver



Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a time range you can explain. Compare with the closest Grafana dashboard, APM UI, or Splunk monitoring console, and spot-check a few raw rows for fields that matter.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix (app × member version), Table (mismatched apps).
grandmaExplanation: We watch platform health and capacity for search, indexing, and data delivery so the system we use for answers does not go dark or slow first.
knownFalsePositives: Cluster and resource noise from rolling work, restarts, and heavy jobs. We use health rollups and change windows to separate flapping from a break.
